title: aglaia korthalsii
intro: Aglaia korthalsii, also known as the Java aglaia or the Malayan champaca, is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Aglaia korthalsii include its ability to treat fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as its potential anti-cancer properties. Aglaia korthalsii can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
